frameworks.name: serial vs parallel attention
frameworks.one_line: Tests whether top-down attention cycles serially or distributes in parallel

frameworks.name: time-resolved fMRI
frameworks.one_line: Uses fast TR (~90ms) BOLD to track relative timing across extrastriate sites

frameworks.name: rhythmic sampling of attention
frameworks.one_line: Cited prior evidence that covert attention may be rapidly cycled

core_question: Does top-down attention modulate multiple extrastriate representations in parallel or in serial?
key_assumptions: BOLD onset/peak timing reflects underlying neural modulation timing
Simultaneous and sequential luminance increases serve as parallel and serial attention models
Extrastriate quadrant representations are spatially separable in fMRI
manipulations: Simultaneous 400ms 25% luminance increase at all four quadrants
Compared stimulation conditions with an attended condition
key_findings: BOLD onset and peak did not shift across regions for simultaneous stimulation
Peak-minus-onset differences were larger for sequential and attended than simultaneous conditions
Reversing sequential stimulation order reversed BOLD onset/peak order in visual cortex
interpretations: Top-down attention acts across multiple extrastriate representations in serial
Highly time-resolved fMRI can resolve relative timing of signal across brain regions
